demographics.living_situation: Lives alone in a two-storey split-level home with internal stairs and poor ambient lighting; previously fully independent, a neighbour is present tonight

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I completely lost my footing on the stairs because the lights were blinding me, and everything went blurry and yellow; I'm terrified to move around my own house.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She nearly went down the internal stairs tonight when the hallway light blinded her and everything blurred, and now she is too frightened to get out of her chair — her neighbour called for help.

symptom_narrative.onset: Vision has been slowly getting worse over about two years; tonight's fright with the stairs came on suddenly under the harsh hallway light
symptom_narrative.duration: Roughly two years of gradual change; the near-miss on the stairs was tonight
symptom_narrative.character: Painless, gradual blurring; strong glare and big halos around bright lights; colours look faded with a yellowish tint; fine print in dim light is very hard
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: She says she can't tell where the edges of the steps are any more and it terrifies her; the glare from the hallway light was blinding
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: Both eyes, all of her vision
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: Steadily worse over two years; much worse in low light and with bright lights or oncoming headlights at night
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Softer, indirect lighting helps a little; new glasses prescriptions have not helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: Harsh overhead lights, night-time, oncoming car headlights, and low-contrast surfaces like the wooden stairs
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: Halos around lights, faded/yellow colours, needing new glasses often without relief; she denies any eye pain, double vision, or seeing a curtain come down over one eye
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: No sudden episodes; this has been a slow, steady decline with repeated changes of glasses
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: Was independent; now confined to the armchair tonight, too frightened to walk; has been struggling to drive at night because of the headlight glare

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Lives alone in a split-level home with steep, uncarpeted wooden internal stairs and harsh overhead hallway lighting with little contrast between the steps; a neighbour checks on her and is with her tonight; she still drives, mainly locally, but finds night driving frightening because of glare

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: Frightened of losing her independence and of being told she can no longer live alone in her own home

objective_data_offered.value: Sitting rigidly in an armchair in a dimly lit room, appears shaken and anxious, squinting hard and shielding her eyes when a light is pointed near her
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: brief phone video in poor lighting; not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: Neighbour says her eyesight has been getting worse for a couple of years and tonight she nearly went down the stairs and now won't get out of the chair

objective_data_offered.timing: just now, read off her home monitor by the neighbour
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home cuff read aloud by a neighbour; not a clinician measurement
